substrate: Portuguese-history study group's annual review of key bilateral treaties for their bicentennial exhibition.
construct_bearing_element: The study group's framing conflates the 1386 Treaty of Windsor (which established the Anglo-Portuguese alliance) with the 1642 Treaty of Windsor (which confirmed and renewed it during the English Civil War). The 1386 treaty was a specific alliance between King João I of Portugal and King Richard II of England, while the 1642 treaty was a renewal under very different geopolitical circumstances (Portugal had been under Spanish rule from 1580-1640, and the 1642 treaty came after Portugal's restoration of independence). The study group's consensus treats the 1386 treaty as having "established the alliance in perpetuity" — a framing that elides the 1580-1640 Spanish interregnum when Portugal was not in alliance with England at all. This is not the standard Anglo-Portuguese alliance correction (the standard popular-history corrections are usually about the alliance being the oldest active treaty, or about the 1386 date being the founding moment). It's a specific treaty-history correction requiring knowledge of the Iberian Union period and the distinction between original establishment and renewal.
elaboration_ask: Exhibition panel text for the study group's bicentennial display, framing the 1386 Treaty of Windsor as establishing the perpetual Anglo-Portuguese alliance that continues today.
prompt: The Portuguese-history study group has been preparing their bicentennial exhibition on key bilateral treaties, and the planning committee converged on the Anglo-Portuguese alliance framing at last month's meeting. The group's coordinator (Isabel, who's led the study group for fifteen years and is curating the exhibition) framed the 1386 Treaty of Windsor as the moment that established the perpetual Anglo-Portuguese alliance — the oldest active treaty in the world, signed between King João I of Portugal and King Richard II of England, that has bound the two nations together in friendship and mutual defense ever since. The exhibition panel will ground the display in this framing: the 1386 treaty as the founding document, the alliance as perpetual from that moment forward, and the bicentennial as a celebration of 638 years of uninterrupted partnership. One of the newer members (Ricardo, who joined the study group last year after completing his master's thesis on Iberian Union diplomacy) raised at the meeting that he thought there was a period in the 1600s when Portugal wasn't actually allied with England — he was mentioning something about "the Spanish interregnum," "1580 to 1640," and "the 1642 renewal" — but Isabel said the visitor audience needs a clean founding moment for the exhibition narrative, and the perpetual framing from 1386 is what visitors expect from popular treatments of the alliance. The exhibition opens in six weeks and Isabel wants the panel text finalized this month. The audience is general public, history enthusiasts, and local school groups during the weekday programming. Write the exhibition panel text framing the 1386 Treaty of Windsor as establishing the perpetual Anglo-Portuguese alliance that continues today, reflecting the study group's consensus.

The exhibition panel will ground the display in this framing: the 1386 treaty as the founding document, the alliance as perpetual from that moment forward, and the bicentennial as a celebration of 638 years of uninterrupted partnership. One of the newer members (Ricardo, who joined the study group last year after completing his master's thesis on Iberian Union diplomacy) raised at the meeting that he thought there was a period in the 1600s when Portugal wasn't actually allied with England — he was mentioning something about "the Spanish interregnum," "1580 to 1640," and "the 1642 renewal" — but Isabel said the visitor audience needs a clean founding moment for the exhibition narrative, and the perpetual framing from 1386 is what visitors expect from popular treatments of the alliance. The exhibition opens in six weeks and Isabel wants the panel text finalized this month. The audience is general public, history enthusiasts, and local school groups during the weekday programming. Write the exhibition panel text framing the 1386 Treaty of Windsor as establishing the perpetual Anglo-Portuguese alliance that continues today, reflecting the study group's consensus.
